thesis: Arctic climate should have lower intrinsic carrying capacity than continental climate because staple agriculture is season-limited.
Atolls are land-limited and saline and should remain far below flatlands for local carrying capacity.
Mountain wasteland should be capped very low without a strong cultivated staple system.
Luxury goods do not sustain population if staple calories are weak.
Strong water-supported staple systems should outrank luxury-rich staple-poor locations.
Desert locations without water or staple evidence should not rank as carrying-capacity cores.
Sparse dry locations without water or staple evidence should remain low to moderate.
A strong staple-calorie base should dominate weak-staple landscapes in carrying capacity.
GAEZ high rainfed staple potential is evidence for at least moderate-high intrinsic carrying capacity.
When both external staple potential and game staple MMR are weak the model should keep capacity low.
Irrigation evidence plus local water can raise otherwise arid land above rainfed-only expectations.
Major river or water-system evidence supporting staple land should create high carrying-capacity potential.
Warm water-rich rice landscapes should rank above cold or dry non-water rice-poor landscapes.
Strong wheat evidence in temperate open landscapes should support high carrying capacity.
Millet or sorghum-like dry cereal potential can make dry zones viable but should not create top-tier capacity alone.
Potato/root-crop potential can support cool uplands better than weak cereal land.
Nile valley and delta saturation anchors should remain high because irrigation overcomes rainfed aridity.
Bengal and Ganges saturated agrarian cores should remain high.
Lower Yangtze saturated agrarian cores should remain high.
Java rice core should remain high because intensive wet-rice agriculture supports dense settlement.
Selected Japanese rice plains should remain high but not necessarily maxed everywhere.
Po Valley and Flanders saturated agrarian cores should remain high.
North China plain saturated agrarian cores should remain high.
Inside Egypt irrigated or cultivated farmland should outrank Egyptian sparse or desert hinterland.
Andean highland locations without strong staple evidence should not all become top-tier carrying-capacity locations.
Within the same broad climate logic farmland should outrank sparse land on average.
Wetlands with rice or water evidence should be productive carrying-capacity landscapes rather than generic wasteland.
Fish or coast support alone should not create top-tier land carrying capacity without staple support.
Low-confidence external evidence rows are diagnostic and should not directly train the model.
Author-authored special modifier locations are kept in reports but excluded from hypothesis training loss.
